events.title: Brainrot Lecture: The Psychology of Attention in a Digital World w/ Dr. Sarah Shepherd
events.description:
The dominant narrative suggests that we’re all headed towards brain rot, but the research tells a more interesting story than that.

The platforms that most of us spend hours on every day are leveraging decades of research on attention to keep us scrolling. What does that mean for how we learn from and interact with the world? Are our attention spans getting shorter? Can you ‘reset’ your attention?

The attention-grabbing strategies used across platforms affect us all—not just your chronically online bestie, but also your roommate that reverted to a flip phone last year. This lecture will tackle some of the big questions many of us are grappling with as we navigate digital environments engineered to capture our attention. We’ll dive into the psychology of attention, and what happens when it encounters systems engineered to capture it. Come for the research, stay for the questions you've probably been asking yourself at 1 am mid-scroll.

Join us for a lecture on Brainrot: The Psychology of Attention in a Digital World w/ Dr. Sarah Shepherd

About the Lecturer 

Sarah Shepherd earned her PhD in Psychology at UC Berkeley, where she investigated how attention and learning is shaped by digital media. Shepherd uses eye-tracking experiments and computational modeling across development and species to answer questions about how the structure of information in the environment shapes attention. Shepherd’s research on attention and learning is published in peer-reviewed venues. Several of her lines of work were so influential, they’ve been adopted for use by Common Sense Media as a publicly available tool to help parents discern the quality of content that they show their children. Shepherd was recently awarded funding to develop an open-source AI toolkit for media analysis by the UC Berkeley Economy and Society Technology Cluster. Prestigious academic institutions and technologists alike have invited Shepherd to present her work at the intersection of psychology and technology. Shepherd has given talks at international venues including Max Planck Institutes in Berlin and Leipzig, the Central European University in Budapest and Vienna, Brown University, Bay Area artificial intelligence incubators, Big Brain SF, and is a repeat guest lecturer at UC Berkeley.

events.title: Punk Software Hack Night: Build Tools That Work for You
events.description:
Software used to be a tool you owned. Now it's mostly something that's done to you: apps that harvest your data, platforms that lock you in, feeds designed to monetize the gap between your intentions and your attention. Punk software is the alternative. Software that's loyal to you. Tools you build, own, understand, and can change.

As Kanjun Qiu, Imbue CEO, puts it: “You don’t have to wait for policymakers anymore, because cheap AI coding means you can now choose, build, and tear down the software in your own life.”

This hack night is for anyone who’s unhappy with the way tech and AI is going and wants to do something about it. You do not need to be a programmer; we'll teach you how to use AI tools. Come if you code, design, write, organize, research, make spreadsheets, break workflows, hate your current tools, or have ever thought: there has to be a better way to do this.

Bring a laptop, a charger, and join the punk software movement.

How the night works:

5:30pm — Doors open. Come in, grab food and drinks, meet people, and settle in.

6:00pm — Kickoff + idea jam. Kanjun will share why we care about punk software and what’s newly possible with AI coding tools, then we’ll do a short activity to help people meet, surface problems worth building for, and choose a direction for the night.

6:20pm — Build time. Pick something and make it.
Technical? Great.

Non-technical? Also great. We’ll help you get set up with AI coding tools and find a way in.

8:30pm — Optional Show & tell. Work-in-progress demos. Half-finished, janky, and held together with duct tape is very much the spirit of the thing.

—

Imbue is an AI company building software that belongs to you. We’re building toward an open agent ecosystem where you can understand, modify, and verify AI agents, so they only work in your interest. Learn about our work and what we stand for: www.imbue.com

events.title: The Epic of… Will O'Brien (Ulysses)
events.description:
We're kicking off a new monthly series. The concept is simple: we put a founder who's building something straight out of a sci-fi novel on stage, break down their grand vision, and let a live crowd decide if it actually holds water.

Our first guest is Will O'Brien, co-founder of Ulysses. They build cheap, autonomous ocean robots. They’re starting off by restoring seagrass meadows (which trap carbon faster than rainforests), but the plan is to tackle any part of the ocean economy that still relies on expensive human crews. They’ve already got real revenue, five generations of vehicles, and they name their robots after sea monsters. The company is literally called Ulysses, so the epic branding writes itself.

The format is a live narrative stress-test. We'll spend 45 minutes with Will unpacking the actual reality of his business: the vision, the team he's recruiting, the skeptics he has to convince, and whatever is most likely to kill the company.

After that, it's on you. The audience gets to grill him with the hardest questions. We take a vote at the start of the night, and another at the end to see if he actually won you over. That shift in the room is the whole point of the event.

Come hang out if you're a founder, an engineer who'd rather build physical robots than another chatbot, someone who invests in hard problems, or if you just want to watch someone defend their life's work in real time.

📍 A space in the Mission, San Francisco (address sent on RSVP) 🗓️ July 8th, doors 5:30, we start 6:00 🎟️ ~60 seats, curated. Recorded and clipped.

We help top founders explore & start new companies in Physical AI through a powerful network of peers, a dedicated hardware lab, and founder-led support – often before we ever write a check. events.title: San Francisco, Uncovered: Things Born in SF
events.description:
🌉 Some of America's most surprising firsts happened in San Francisco.

Long before Silicon Valley, San Francisco was shaping the world in unexpected ways. The city's streets hold the stories behind everything from the martini and the first underground parking garage to the founding of the United Nations and the birth of topless dancing. Yet most of these stories are hiding in plain sight.

In this lecture, Hannah Blazei, journalist, KPIX News editor, and creator of Good Stories San Francisco, uncovers the stories behind some of San Francisco's most iconic things. She brings to life the people, moments, and unlikely events that helped make San Francisco unlike anywhere else in the world.

One story is still unwritten... because you'll choose it! Before the lecture, we'll ask our community what San Francisco story they'd like to hear about. It could be a historic event, an iconic landmark, a neighborhood mystery, or anything else that makes this city special. The comment with the most likes across our social media will become Hannah's final story of the evening.

Voting is officially open! Comment on any of our social media posts to cast your vote!

events.title: Film Screening: The Last Taboo
events.description:
The World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States is in full swing. 

Out of 500,000 active male professional soccer players worldwide, fewer than ten are openly gay. While homosexuality becomes less scrutinized in other spheres of life, the subject remains a near-complete taboo in professional soccer.

The feature-length documentary The Last Taboo gives a voice to those who have broken this silence, allowing them to tell their deeply personal stories. While each biography could not be more different, the personal experiences they depict — the conflicts faced between self-denial and liberation — create a familiar pattern. The film leaves us with lingering questions:

Why is this subject so completely hushed up within the world of soccer, in particular? Is there a fear that it might throw a wrench into the gears of soccer's high-octane marketing machine? Or are players and fans already far ahead of those currently at the controls?

The Last Taboo delves into these questions, telling not only the story of the players but also shedding light on a society in transition — a society that, perhaps, is not always quite as tolerant as it believed itself to be.

Thank you to our community outreach partner, Frameline. Founded in 1977, Frameline is a San Francisco-based nonprofit media arts organization dedicated to the development, exhibition, distribution, and preservation of LGBTQ+ cinema and independent queer media.

events.title: Sloptimist Etymologies with Adam Aleksic & Daniel Keller
events.description:
Join linguist and internet language researcher Adam Aleksic (Etymology Nerd) and "formal" artist and internet theorist, Daniel Keller (who coined the term sloptimist) for a conversation exploring how AI-generated media, algorithmic abundance, and networked culture are transforming the way language, meaning, and taste evolve.

—

Adam Aleksic is a linguist, writer, and educator best known as the "Etymology Nerd," creating videos about language and internet culture for an audience of more than three million across TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube. He is the author of the New York Times bestselling book Algospeak, which explores how social media is transforming language and communication. A graduate of Harvard College, where he studied linguistics and served as president of the Harvard Undergraduate Linguistics Society, Aleksic has lectured on language and social media at leading universities and is a frequent guest on NBC and NPR. He is currently writing a new book examining how technology is reshaping our sense of reality.

Daniel Keller is a “former artist,” internet theorist, writer, and entrepreneur who has exhibited internationally as a solo artist and as half of Aids-3D, a seminal post-internet collective. He was formerly co-founder of New Models and Channel.xyz. In 2023-2024 he collaborated with and led cultural strategy at Vaporware, an Urbit-ecosystem startup.

—

This event is presented alongside our latest exhibition, The Epistemologies of Slop, ​an exhibition exploring how meaning, trust, and culture are produced under conditions of algorithmic excess and generative media.

events.title: Future of Us: Dogpatch Block Party!
events.description:
Food, music, neighbors, and a whole lot of chalk! This is a day to celebrate the Dogpatch — and to imagine the future of all of our neighborhoods.

Make new friends, explore interactive stations, and add your vision to our chalk art gallery. 

⚡ This event is part of our Neighborhood Futures day. Check out the programming happening at The Pearl just a 10 minute walk away, and if you're a local to the neighborhood, sign up to earn your patches at the first annual Patchwork neighborhood crawl! ⚡

Disclaimer: Permits filed and pending.

About the Event:

The Future of Us is marking the generational milestone of the US's 250th anniversary by coming together to celebrate community visions, creative expression, and civic voice.

We can't celebrate "Neighborhood Futures" without celebrating our home neighborhood! This community block party is inspired by our mission to bring our local neighborhood together in creative ways. We're partnering with the Dogpatch Arts & Business Association and Pier 70's Building 12 to bring the community a day of music, art, craft, food, and beer!

What to Expect: 

Creative Activations

Chalk Art Gallery: 12+ artists are doing a takeover of our street, filling it with visions of what their dream neighborhoods would be! Don't worry, we're holding some spots for you to add your own.

Dogpatch Photo Booth: Get your official Dogpatch portrait, props optional, but always encouraged.

Meet You Neighbors Lounge: Hang out at one of the lounges set-up with conversation prompts and interactions to make it a little easier to spark a connection.

Building 12 Pop-Ups: Check out the pop-up art gallery, artisans, and special events nearby inside Building 12.

Food, Drinks & Entertainment

Food Trucks: Expect Bay-wide and local favorite food trucks, and a few surprise treats.

Great Beer & Views: Enjoy a drink at Standard Deviant next door, with amazing views of the Bay. If you get there early, they'll probably have World Cup on!

Live Music: We'll have music throughout the evening to keep you moving.

Neighborhood Activations

Pop by some of the other festival events happening at the same time on-site and nearby:

"Patchwork": A Neighborhood Crawl: For the people who live and work in the neighborhood, make sure to check out the first ever Patchwork neighborhood crawl. Earn free "patches" and a tote by getting to know your neighborhood! Register here - event starts at 2pm!

"Neighborhood Futures" Event: The festival's signature programming is happening a 10 minute walk away at The Pearl. Check out 100+ original artworks, conversations from local civic builders and leaders on building community and making an impact in your neighborhood, and enjoy a live DJ on the roof.  Register here.

"Sardine Games": From 4-6, head over to an immersive, game-based experience inspired by the Bay's sardine fishery. Register here.

Planning Your Trip:

Parking & Transportation
Street parking is available but limited. We recommend rideshare or public transportation. The Block Party is easy to reach by the Third Street Muni and a short walk from the T-Third line.

Accessibility & Considerations
This is a largely outdoor event, with access to Building 12. If you have specific accommodation questions or requests, please reach out to future@theplenary.co to discuss what's possible.

⚡ About the Future of Us Festival

Launching on America's 250th anniversary, the inaugural Future of Us festival is a 10-day celebration of civic imagination and possibility. We're turning community-sourced, expert-crafted visions of the future into art, storytelling, and participatory experiences. Curated by a coalition of artists, scientists, storytellers, and civic builders, the festival features 100+ events spanning every corner of the city (floral labyrinths, speculative future worlds, dance parties with a purpose, and neighborhood meet-ups), it's a living exploration of what a more creative and generative civic culture could feel like. Organized around four themes (Green Futures, Social Futures, Science & Tech Futures, and Media Futures), Future of Us is breaking down the barriers between art, ideas, and each other, and everyone is invited to co-imagine what comes next.

⚡ Learn More: future-of-us.com
⚡ Check out the Schedule of Events: future-of-us.com
⚡ Follow Us: https://www.instagram.com/futures.us

About the Organization

Future of Us is stewarded by The Plenary, Co., a 501(c)3 civic arts and sciences nonprofit based in Dogpatch, and co-led by a coalition of partners. All donations are tax-deductible. Make a donation here.

events.title: From Seaweed to Shirt: The Chalk Up Tee Launch + Innovation Lab Tour
events.description:
Hi everyone!

We're excited to invite you to Supercarb's first-ever product launch.

Supercarb is a material technology company transforming seaweed and agricultural waste into high-performance textile fibers. Our journey began in a garage in Emeryville, California, where we invented a new seaweed-based fiber platform. That breakthrough was later recognized and supported by Activate, a Berkeley-based nonprofit that empowers scientists and entrepreneurs tackling climate change and other global challenges.

Today, we're taking the next step in that journey.

Join us as we launch the Chalk Up Tee, our first product built using Supercarb's Seaweed Tech™, designed for the climbing and outdoor community.

The vision for the future of textiles

Introduction to the Chalk Up Tee

Try It On + Purchase

Experience the product firsthand

Limited launch-day purchasing opportunity

Innovation Lab Tour

A behind-the-scenes look at how we turn seaweed into textile fibers using physics, chemistry, and a lot of persistence

We're incredibly proud of the work that has gone into bringing this product to life, and we'd love for you to celebrate this milestone with us.

P.S. Our lab is located inside a historic former Dodge manufacturing facility, making for a uniquely industrial backdrop to an evening of science, textiles, and community.

Cheers,

Team Supercarb

What is Local Alignment?

An unstructured hangout for Bay Area interns, engineers, founders, and anyone with a free Saturday. Walk in, pick a table, switch anytime. No forced icebreakers. No business-card rituals. Just people actually talking to each other.

🧠 Deep-Dive Tables (group coffee chat)

No one's interviewing you, no one's pitching you — just conversation.
Click with someone? Keep going. Don't? Switch tables. One afternoon ≈ ten coffee chats.

· 🤖 AI Software: LLMs  Agents / Infra

A few 5-minute talks during snack time. Bell rings, you're done. No one will pitch you for 40 minutes.

Who's coming?

Summer interns, new grads, engineers at FAANG and AI startups, founders, job seekers — and anyone who just wants something real to do on a Saturday.
No mentor sessions, no office hours. But the person you just accused in Werewolf might be a senior on your dream team.

events.title: How to Break into SF as an Immigrant Founder
events.description:
How do you actually break into San Francisco as an immigrant founder?

Moving to SF is exciting...until you realize everyone already seems to know everyone.

How do you build your first network? Meet investors? Find your people? And avoid wasting months learning the same lessons every founder before you?

In this founder-led workshop, we'll unpack what it actually takes to build a startup as an immigrant founder in Silicon Valley.

You'll walk away with:

👉 𝗵𝗼𝘄 𝘁𝗼 𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗳𝗶𝗿𝘀𝘁 𝗻𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸 (the communities, events, and relationships that actually open doors)

👉 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘂𝗻𝘄𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗻 𝗿𝘂𝗹𝗲𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗦𝗶𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗼𝗻 𝗩𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲𝘆 (how networking, fundraising, and founder culture work differently here)

👉 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘆𝗯𝗼𝗼𝗸 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗴𝗮𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗺𝗼𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘂𝗺 (finding customers, mentors, investors, and opportunities without an established network)

Whether you've just landed in SF or are planning the move, you'll leave with practical advice you can use immediately.

This is not a panel. It's a founder-led workshop in a bar, designed to go deep on one topic so you leave with real takeaways.

Come for the lesson, stay for the conversations after.

About the Speaker

Adriel Yong is the Co-Founder and COO of Clout Kitchen (now Clouted), an AI startup building the next generation of creator and distribution intelligence tools. He helped scale the company from inception through its $4.45M seed round backed by a16z, Peak XV’s Surge, AppWorks, and Antler, and later its $7M seed financing led by Slow Ventures. He is also an active angel investor and ecosystem builder, supporting early-stage founders across Southeast Asia and Silicon Valley.

7:00–7:45 PM

​🧠 About Founders on Tap

Founders on Tap is a workshop series in SF's coolest spots.

Each session goes deep on one useful topic, such as community, GTM, fundraising, automation, or getting your first customers.

​​​🔔 Stay in the Loop

📸 Follow us on Instagram

We’ll reach out if your session feels like a good fit.

Yes. You must be 21+ and present a valid ID to enter the venue.

Photos and short video clips may be taken for promotional purposes. By attending, you consent to being photographed or recorded.

​Accessibility

events.title: AI Philosophy Nights: Indifferent Intelligence
events.description:
Join us for the next edition of our monthly AI Philosophy Nights where we attempt communal sense-making.

Each month, we bring together philosophers, historians, technologists, and social scientists for a high-level fireside chat, followed by small-group salon style discussions.

This session: Giving a Damn in the Age of Indifferent Intelligence

The problem with artificial intelligence, as our guest puts it, is that computers don't give a damn. Machines can now write, converse, and optimize but they cannot care. And as AI systems take over more of our work and cater ever more precisely to our preferences, the deeper risk is the slow erosion of our capacity to care, to commit, and to find things worth wanting in the first place.

In this session, our founder Banu Kellner sits down with B. Scot Rousse to explore what care actually is, why it may be the missing foundation of the AI alignment problem, and how we might design technologies to recover our ability to give a damn.

6:30-7:00pm Doors Open. Drinks & light bites will be served. Doors will be closed at 7pm.

7:00-8:00pm Fireside Chat. Banu & B. will examine care in the age of AI.

8:00-9:00pm Small Group Discussions. Break into small-group conversations to grapple with these questions together.

9:00-9:30pm Whole Group Conversation. We will come back together to share insights and further questions.

~~~

B. Scot Rousse ("B") Visiting scholar and lecturer in the Philosophy department Uc Berkeley, and an affiliate researcher at Topos Institute. His work foregrounds the embodied, skillful, and caring dimensions of human life often obscured by computational models of mind and intelligence. He has also been drumming in punk bands his entire life. He writes about philosophy and AI at withoutwhy.substack.com

Banu Kellner is an executive coach to AI Founders building frontier technologies. She is the author of the Superhumanist Manifesto and the founder of the SuperHuman Society, an organization focused on championing a future where technology expands human dignity, capability, and connection.

Sponsored by Imbue. An AI company building tools to empower humans in the AI age. We’re building toward an open agent ecosystem where you can understand, modify, and verify AI agents, so they only work in your interest. Learn about our work and what we stand for: www.imbue.com

events.title: Finding “The One”: Compatibility & Relationship Science
events.description:
❤️ Is there really such a thing as a perfect match?

From dating apps and reality TV to advice from friends and family, we're constantly told what makes two people compatible. But relationship scientists have spent decades studying a surprisingly simple question: what actually predicts whether a relationship will last?

In this lecture, Dr. Annika From, a relationship researcher at the University of Nebraska–Lincoln, explores what psychology research can tell us about romantic compatibility. Drawing on decades of studies, she examines some of the most common beliefs about relationships, including whether opposites attract, how much similarity matters, and whether attraction can grow over time.

This talk separates popular dating myths from scientific evidence and explores what researchers have learned about the factors that truly contribute to happy, healthy, and long-lasting relationships. ✨

events.title: Free Stage Play | The Light in Your Heart and the Way Back Home (by Tiangu Troupe)
events.description:
Introduction | 天鼓剧团 · 东方心灵故事集

‘A single performance can change a life. Let this night kindle the lamp within your heart.’

「只需要一场戏，就能改变人生；让这一夜，成为点亮心灯的一夜。」

Rooted in Eastern wisdom, Tiangu Troupe creates theatre that speaks to the heart. In this special performance, step into three timeless parables of devotion, awakening, and a heart returning home.

天鼓剧团——一个以东方智慧为核心、以触动心灵为使命的戏剧团队。

在这场独家演出中，

我们将带你走入三部跨越时空的东方寓言：

关于至孝、关于智慧、关于心的回归。

These are not mere stories. They are mirrors, reflecting every traveller as they walk through the mirage of this world.

它们不是故事，而是镜子，照见每一个在尘世行走的你。

Whether a student seeking direction, a weary wanderer of city life, a creator in search of clarity, or a soul longing to break free from the quiet repetition of everyday life,
may this evening rekindle a long-forgotten light within you.

无论你是迷惘的学子、疲惫的都市人、正在寻找方向的创作者、

还是渴望从「日复一日的生活」中惊醒的灵魂——

这一夜，将为你点燃那束久违的光。

‘Let us meet in the theatre.
Between shadow and glow, rediscover yourself and set foot on a new path.’

让我们在剧场相遇。

在光影之间，重新认识自己，开启一段新的旅途。

Synopsis | 剧目简介

Story One - Oteru’s Lamp | 《阿照的一灯》

After the passing of her adoptive parents, the orphan Oteru begins a solitary journey across mountains and wilderness to offer a lamp on Mount Kōya, praying for her parents and the people of her village.
A hundred miles of danger, carried by a single flame of devotion.
How will she withstand the storms of fate?
And how can one small lamp, once offered, shine without end?

孤女阿照在养父母离世后，只身翻山越岭前往高野山供灯，为养父母与乡亲祈福。

百里险途，一盏心灯相随。

她将如何穿越命运的风雨？

而那盏小小供灯，又如何得以长明不熄？

Story Two - The Barber | 《理发师》

In ancient India, where the caste system casts long shadows, a humble barber hears rumour of a wisdom said to quiet suffering and open the gates of understanding.
Unfazed by mockery or status, he follows only the yearning of a heart seeking liberation.
Can someone born into poverty truly reach the vastness of real wisdom?

在等级森严的古印度，一位出身低微的理发师听闻世间有物，能息诸烦恼、启诸智慧。

他不惧嘲讽，不问身份，只凭渴求解脱的心向光而行。

贫贱如他，能否触及真正的智慧？

Story Three - The Poor Son Returns Home | 《穷子回家》

A wealthy man’s son leaves home in search of “true happiness.”
Years of wandering reduce him to exhaustion, his purpose forgotten, his path home erased.
Fate reunites him with the father who has never stopped searching for him.
Where does true happiness dwell?
And could “returning home” mean returning to something that was never lost?

富家少年离家追寻「真正的快乐」。

多年漂泊，他形容枯槁，也失了归途。

命运让他与苦苦寻找他的父亲重逢。

真正的快乐究竟在哪里？

而「回家」是否意味着回到那从未失落的地方？

As part of our charitable mission, this performance is completely free—an offering of light to the community.

本场演出为公益性质，完全免费。

Date & Time: Friday, 7th Aug,2026  19:30
Run Time: 90 minutes, no interval
Language: Performed in Chinese with English subtitles

Venue: Presidio Theatre, 99 Moraga Avenue San Francisco, CA 94129

Producer: Tiangu Troupe

时间： 2026年8月7日（星期五）19:30

时长： 90分钟，无中场休息

语言： 中文演出 · 英文字幕

地点： Presidio Theatre, 99 Moraga Avenue San Francisco, CA 94129

出品： 天鼓剧团

Tiangu Troupe Introduction

Founded in 2017, Tiangu Troupe is a non-profit theatre ensemble dedicated to sharing uplifting, heart-centered stories with audiences around the world. Over the past eight years, the troupe has toured across San Francisco, Los Angeles, London, Cambridge, Auckland, Sydney, Osaka, and more—bringing over twenty performances to life, each warmly received and deeply appreciated.

Guided by a charitable mission, Tiangu Troupe continues to offer free performances, ensuring that anyone—regardless of background—can step into a theatre, breathe, listen, and rediscover the light within.

剧团简介

天鼓剧团成立于2017年，是一个公益性质的话剧团队，致力于为大众带来温暖、启发心灵的故事。过去八年间，剧团巡演足迹遍及旧金山、洛杉矶、伦敦、剑桥、奥克兰、悉尼、大坂等地，共演出二十余场，场场好评如潮。

作为公益剧团，天鼓始终坚持以免费演出的方式，让所有观众都能走进剧场、触及心中的光。
